description:
To properly set the group owner of /etc/selinux, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/selinux
rationale:
The ownership of the /etc/selinux directory by the root group is important
because this directory hosts SELinux configuration. Protection of this
directory is critical for system security. Assigning the ownership to root
ensures exclusive control of the SELinux configuration.
ocil_clause:
/etc/selinux does not have a group owner of
root
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /etc/selinux, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/selinux
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: root
fixtext:
Change the group of the file /etc/selinux to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/selinux
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/selinux file must be group-owned by root.
title: Verify Group Who Owns /etc/selinux Directory
